---
description: "Use when adding, editing, or reviewing translation message files. Covers the single-locale rule, key naming, structure consistency, and how translations flow into components."
applyTo: "messages/**/*.json"
---

# i18n / Translation Guidelines

## The Golden Rule

The site is **English-only**. There is only one message file:

- `messages/en.json` — the single source of truth for all user-visible text

`messages/it.json` and `messages/ru.json` have been removed. Do not recreate them.

## Key Naming

- camelCase keys, nested inside a section object.
- Section name matches the component/page it belongs to (lowercase).

```json
{
  "hero": {
    "h1": "...",
    "subtitle": "..."
  },
  "services": {
    "heading": "...",
    "cards": [...]
  }
}
```

- Arrays are allowed for repeated items (e.g. service cards, process steps).
- Never use dot-separated flat keys at the top level.

## Adding a New Section or Page

1. Add the new key block to `en.json` (write the copy in British English).
2. Never create parallel locale files.

## Using Translations in Components

Server component:
```tsx
import { useTranslations } from "next-intl";
const t = useTranslations("sectionName");
return <h1>{t("heading")}</h1>;
```

Client component: same import. The component must render inside the next-intl provider (already wired globally in `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx`).

For typed array iteration:
```tsx
{(t.raw("cards") as Array<{ title: string; body: string }>).map(...)}
```

## Current Section Keys (en.json)

| Key | Used in |
|-----|---------|
| `metadata` | `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` |
| `header` | `Header.tsx` |
| `hero` | `Hero.tsx` |
| `stats` | `Stats.tsx` |
| `services` | `Services.tsx` |
| `founder` | `Founder.tsx` |
| `about` | `About.tsx` |
| `process` | `Process.tsx` |
| `ctaStrip` | `CtaStrip.tsx` |
| `contact` | `Contact.tsx` |
| `newsletter` | `NewsletterSignup.tsx` |
| `footer` | `Footer.tsx` |
| `contactPage` | `src/app/[locale]/contact/page.tsx` |
| `cookieBanner` | `CookieBanner.tsx` |
